bio: Saeed Khatibzadeh is an Iranian diplomat who served as spokesman for the [Ministry of Foreign Affairs](/orgs/ministry-of-foreign-affairs) from 2020 to 2021 and was appointed head of the [Institute for Political and International Studies (IPIS)](/orgs/ipis) in January 2025. While stationed in Berlin in 2014, Khatibzadeh conceived and organised the [Iran Experts Initiative](/events/iei-email-leak-2023), a covert Foreign Ministry influence operation that recruited Western-based Iranian academics to promote Tehran's nuclear negotiating positions. In a March 2014 email to IPIS head [Mostafa Zahrani](/people/mostafa-zahrani), he proposed building a network of 'second-generation Iranians' affiliated with Western think tanks and offered them 'political support.' He recruited [Ariane Tabatabai](/people/ariane-tabatabai) and [Dina Esfandiary](/people/dina-esfandiary) as the core group following a meeting in Prague. In April 2015, he reported the IEI's media metrics to [Mohammad Javad Zarif](/people/javad-zarif) and the negotiating team. The operation was exposed when leaked emails were published by Iran International and Semafor in September 2023.
editorial_notes: Mastermind of IEI. Appointed IPIS head Jan 2025 per Iran International.
